headline: Iranian Missile Strikes Are Costing Big Oil Billions in Lost Revenue
summary: Damaged energy infrastructure is likely to take years to come back online, but a surge in oil-and-gas prices is helping to offset some of the lost production for now.

content.title: Iranian Missile Strikes Are Costing Big Oil Billions in Lost Revenue
content.summary: Damaged energy infrastructure is likely to take years to come back online, but a surge in oil-and-gas prices is helping to offset some of the lost production for now.

headline: Iranian Missile Strikes Are Costing Big Oil Billions in Lost Revenue
summary: When Iranian missiles struck the Pearl gas-to-liquids facility in Qatar, they knocked out one of Shell’s crown jewels, a giant plant that is among the most sophisticated and profitable businesses in the company’s sprawling global operations.  The plant was so heavily damaged that one of its two production lines is expected to be shut for at least a year, Qatar said.  Exxon Mobil, which has more at stake in Qatar than any other big oil company, gets roughly one-fifth of its oil-and-gas production from the Middle East, analysts estimate.

headline: Exxon Mobil Weighs Alaska Expansion Against Qatar Damage And Valuation Signals
summary: Exxon Mobil participated in a record-breaking lease sale in Alaska, securing access to new acreage in a highly contested auction. The Alaska lease auction set new records for revenue and acreage, signaling strong industry interest in U.S. energy assets despite ongoing legal challenges. Iranian missile strikes damaged LNG and gas-to-liquids facilities in Qatar where Exxon Mobil has material exposure, with repairs expected to take years. The combination of new Alaska leases and prolonged...
